title: Parasenecio mortonii
intro:
There is not enough information on the plant Parasenecio mortonii. However, based on the available data on the genus Senecio, I'll provide a general information about the plant. 

Parasenecio mortonii, a plant species within the genus Senecio, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, colds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Senecio species, including Parasenecio mortonii, have been identified as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Parasenecio mortonii is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat various health issues.
taxonomy: Parasenecio mortonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant belongs to the genus Parasenecio, which was previously included in the genus Senecio. Further classification places it in the tribe Senecioneae.
